title: NASA And UFO ETFs Are Surging Ahead Of SpaceX IPO But Key Risks Remain
description: The Procure Space ETF (NASDAQ: UFO ) and Tema Space Innovators (NYSE: NASA ) are performing strongly amid the ongoing SpaceX IPO frenzy, with both funds reaching record highs of $68 and $43, respectively, as assets under management continue to climb. Still, these funds face some major risks as the IPO nears. NASA vs UFO ETFs | Source: TradingView UFO and NASA ETFs Have Soared Ahead of the SpaceX IPO Top space ETFs have done well this year, driven by the ongoing surge in top companies in the industry. Rocket Lab (NASDAQ: RKLB ) has soared by over 436% in the last 12 months, while Planet Labs (NYSE: PL ) jumped by 766%. Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ: LUNR ), AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ: ASTS ), and EchoStar (NASDAQ: SATS ) have all soared by over 300% in the last 12 months. These ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

title: White House Literally Made a Fake UFO Website Just to Track Immigrants
description: The White House's new immigration website, Aliens.gov, uses sci-fi imagery, sparking criticism for potentially dehumanising migrants. The site also features a live dashboard displaying immigration enforcement data.

title: What Is Hidden in the Exploding Fireball Reports From Pentagon UFO Leak
description: Decades of once‐secret US military reports, videos and witness accounts reveal strange glowing objects in the sky that officials have logged but still struggle to explain.

title: Trump urged to share all UFO files as lawmaker group moves to Congress
description: Group calls U.S. government to release all UFO filesDespite the U.S. government releasing a series of UFO files batch, there is a section of public not convinced.Instead, they are calling for a full disclosure of extraterrestrials.It included a group of lawmakers and an ex-Pentagon whistleblower,...

title: Ex-Pentagon Official: UAP Files Expose 'Non-Human' Technology and Government Cover-Up Going Back to 1940s
description: Luis Elizondo, a former Pentagon official, claims newly released UAP files reveal 'non-human' technology and decades of government cover-up, sparking debate over transparency and national security.
